macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Nasdaq Hits Fifth Consecutive Losing Session Amid AI Selloff
items.summary: Technology stocks, particularly AI winners, pulled back sharply as the Nasdaq recorded its fifth consecutive losing session; semiconductor stocks remained under pressure amid concerns that OpenAI may delay its IPO, slowing AI infrastructure spending.
items.source: YouTube (Jobs Report, AI Selloff & How To Make Money This Week)

items.title: Defensive Sectors Gain as Market Leadership Shifts Away from Tech
items.summary: Money flowed into defensive sectors including healthcare, financials, consumer staples, and utilities, highlighting a meaningful shift in market leadership despite improving inflation expectations and softer oil prices limiting broader losses.
items.source: YouTube (Jobs Report, AI Selloff & How To Make Money This Week)

items.title: Fed Tightening Possibility Weighed as June Jobs Report Looms
items.summary: Investors are weighing the possibility of additional Federal Reserve tightening later this year ahead of the critical June Jobs Report on Thursday, July 2, which will be the biggest catalyst for interest rate outlooks.
items.source: YouTube (Jobs Report, AI Selloff & How To Make Money This Week)

items.title: Global Economy Exposed to Renewed Inflationary Pressure from Shipping Disruptions
items.summary: Despite oil prices calming down, the global economy remains exposed to higher energy and shipping costs as a ceasefire in a key shipping route is under serious strain, with two commercial vessels struck recently and US-Iran military activity escalating.

items.title: Analyst Predicts Inflationary Boom Over Deflationary Bust Amid US Fiscal Stimulus
items.summary: An analyst argues a deflationary bust is least likely due to massive US fiscal stimulus (budget deficits of ~7% of GDP) and a tsunami of private AI capex, suggesting the highest odds are for an inflationary boom if oil stays below $100–$120.
items.source: YouTube (There's Going To Be One Hell Of A Hangover When The ...)

items.title: Earnings Watch: Nike, Constellation Brands, and General Mills to Report
items.summary: With earnings season remaining light, companies including Nike, Constellation Brands, General Mills, FactSet, and Progress Software are set to report, offering insight into consumer spending and enterprise demand.
items.source: YouTube (Jobs Report, AI Selloff & How To Make Money This Week)

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Slips Below $60K Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions as Market Cap Falls 3.4%
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near $59,400–$60,000, dropping 0.5%–1.05% over 24 hours as renewed U.S.–Iran confrontations triggered sharp selling pressure, pushing the global crypto market cap to $2.02 trillion after a 3.38% decline[1][2][3].

items.title: Over $160M in Crypto Liquidations Hit Longs as Fear & Greed Index Plunges to Extreme Fear
items.summary: The crypto market recorded approximately $161 million in liquidations over 24 hours, overwhelmingly from long positions, while the Fear & Greed Index fell to 17, signaling 'Extreme Fear' amid institutional selling and weakening derivatives[2][3][7].

items.title: EU Accelerates MiCA Enforcement with 12.5% Penalty Framework Ahead of July 1 Licensing Deadline
items.summary: The European Banking Authority published a proposed penalty framework under MiCA, imposing fines up to 12.5% of annual revenue or twice violation profits for significant token issuers, just before the July 1 licensing deadline for EU crypto firms[1].

items.title: U.S. House Advances Temporary CBDC Ban Through 2030 to President Trump for Signature
items.summary: House Speaker Mike Johnson confirmed that legislation containing a temporary ban on central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) through 2030 will be sent to President Donald Trump for signature on Monday, marking a major regulatory shift[1].

items.title: BIS Warns Stablecoins Lack Core Money Traits as American Express Hires Stablecoin VP
items.summary: The Bank for International Settlements (BIS) argued stablecoins still lack resilience and interoperability, while American Express signaled growing institutional interest by opening a search for a Vice President of Stablecoin and Blockchain Strategy[1].

items.title: Arthur Hayes Buys $2.2M SYN Tokens, Claims Hypercall Could Compete with Deribit
items.summary: Arthur Hayes purchased 6.16 million SYN tokens valued at $2.2 million and stated that Hypercall could become a serious competitor to Deribit in the decentralized options market, highlighting notable project developments[1].

items.title: Loopring Shuts Down DEX After Failing Adoption, Returns Assets to Ethereum Layer 1
items.summary: Loopring announced the shutdown of its decentralized exchange due to lack of meaningful adoption, returning eligible user assets directly to Ethereum Layer 1 wallets with the team covering all gas fees[1].

items.title: South Korea’s New Listing Rules Take Effect July 1, Pressuring KOSDAQ Crypto Treasury Firms
items.summary: South Korea’s updated listing rules will take effect on July 1, increasing delisting risks for KOSDAQ-listed crypto treasury companies if market capitalization continues to decline[1].

items.title: Hyper Foundation Grants $10M to Migrate Projects from Discontinued USDH Stablecoin
items.summary: The Hyper Foundation committed approximately $10 million in grants to help projects migrate away from the discontinued USDH stablecoin before the end of July, addressing a critical infrastructure gap[1].

items.title: Galaxy Digital CEO Novogratz: Bitcoin’s Short-Term Direction Uncertain Amid Strategy Financing Watch
items.summary: Galaxy Digital CEO Mike Novogratz stated Bitcoin’s short-term direction remains uncertain as investors closely watch Strategy’s financing model, reflecting ongoing institutional caution[1].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta AI Glasses Launch with Automatic Face-Scanning Code, Sparking Privacy Concerns
items.summary: Meta's new AI-powered glasses include code that automatically scans and saves facial data, raising significant privacy and ethical questions as AI becomes embedded in consumer devices.

items.title: OpenAI Unveils Jalapeño Chip, Highlighting Cost as Critical Factor in AI Model Viability
items.summary: OpenAI's new Jalapeño chip demonstrates that the operational cost of running AI models is becoming as important as their intelligence, signaling a shift in industry priorities.

items.title: ManpowerGroup Survey: Broken Recruiting Processes and Fragmented Systems Block AI Gains
items.summary: A new survey reveals that isolated tools, siloed data, and fragmented recruiting systems are preventing companies from fully realizing AI benefits, according to ManpowerGroup Talent Solutions and Everest Group.

items.title: AI Safety Debate Reverses: Advanced Models Now Launched with Stricter Safeguards and Government Oversight
items.summary: The most advanced AI models are increasingly released with limited access, stricter safeguards, export controls, and security reviews, marking a full-circle shift in the AI safety debate.

items.title: AI Becomes Institutional: Shift from Product Race to Integration in Courts, Governments, and Defense Systems
items.summary: AI is no longer just a product race but is becoming embedded in courts, governments, defense systems, and consumer devices, with companies now competing over compute, talent, regulation, and data.

items.title: Midjourney Medical Tests Whole-Body Imaging for Routine Consumer Wellness Visits
items.summary: Midjourney Medical is exploring whether whole-body imaging can become a standard part of routine consumer wellness visits, advancing AI applications in clinical and preventive healthcare.

items.title: New Machine Learning Papers on Adversarial Contamination, Synthetic Data, and Federated Learning Published on arXiv
items.summary: Recent arXiv submissions include breakthroughs in iterative algorithms with signal adaptivity, synthetic data augmentation for imbalanced classification, and re-labeling strategies in federated learning.

items.title: Workday Faces California AI Discrimination Claims Despite Nonresident Status
items.summary: A federal judge ruled that Workday's California headquarters creates a sufficient nexus to apply state AI discrimination laws even to nonresidents, intensifying regulatory scrutiny on AI hiring tools.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Live as November 19 Release Date Confirmed
items.summary: Rockstar Games has enabled pre-orders for GTA 6, signaling strong confidence in hitting the November 19, 2026 release date for the highly anticipated title.

items.title: Bungie Announces Layoffs Impacting Most of Destiny Team
items.summary: Bungie has confirmed layoffs with significant cuts affecting the majority of the Destiny development team, marking a major shift in the studio's structure.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Remake Launches July 9 with Improved Havana City
items.summary: The Assassin's Creed Black Flag remake is set for release on July 9, featuring a remastered Havana city with enhanced NPCs and visuals, with reviews dropping July 8.

items.title: Echoes of Onrad and Black Flag Resynced Release July 9 Across Major Platforms
items.summary: Two new titles, Echoes of Onrad and Black Flag Resynced, are launching on July 9 for PC, Xbox, and PlayStation, joining a busy July release schedule.

items.title: Halo Campaign Evolved Remaster Launches July 28 for PC, Xbox, and PlayStation
items.summary: The original Halo game is receiving another remaster titled Halo Campaign Evolved, launching July 28 across PC, Xbox, and PlayStation platforms.

items.title: Doom: The Dark Ages Receives Revelations Campaign DLC on July 7
items.summary: A new campaign expansion called Revelations is coming to Doom: The Dark Ages on July 7, adding significant content to the game.

items.title: Major Titles Fable, Tomb Raider, and Persona 4 Planned for February 2027
items.summary: The early 2027 release window is filling up with Fable, Tomb Raider Legacy of Atlantis, and Persona 4 Revival all planned for February, alongside potential Law Fay launch.

items.title: Senator Aquino Rejects Blanket Ban on Video Games Amid Campus Violence Concerns
items.summary: Senator Bam Aquino has called for strict regulations rather than a blanket ban on video games, rejecting the idea amid recent reports of campus violence.

items.title: Steam Summer Sale 2026 Features Civilization VI at 90% Off
items.summary: The Steam Summer Sale 2026 is underway, with Civilization VI highlighted as the best value deal at 90% off, bringing the base game to approximately $5-$6.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple, Microsoft, and Sony Announce Major Price Hikes on MacBook, iPad, and PS5 Due to Memory Shortage
items.summary: Apple is raising MacBook Pro prices by $300 and iPad prices significantly, while Microsoft's Surface Pro jumps from $999 to $1,599, and Sony's PS5 and other electronics face across-the-board increases driven by a 600% surge in DRAM memory costs.

items.title: iPhone 18 Expected to See $200 Price Increase Ahead of Unannounced Launch
items.summary: Industry estimates predict the upcoming iPhone 18 will cost $200 more than previous models, rising from $10.99 to $12.99, as part of a broader trend of inflationary price hikes across consumer electronics due to semiconductor and memory chip shortages.

items.title: Semiconductor Industry Surges Over 200% in First Half of 2026 Amid Memory Chip Inflation
items.summary: Semiconductor stocks have more than doubled in the first six months of 2026, with DRAM memory prices hitting a historic 246% increase, creating inflationary pressure on tech products despite AI's long-term deflationary expectations.
